title: [InstCombine] Handle commuted cases of the fold `((B|C)&A)|B -> B|(A&C)`
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by expanding pattern matching to handle commutative operations and swapped operand positions. The original pattern `((B | C) & A) | B -> B | (A & C)` only matched when the `or` was the left operand. The patch adds `m_c_And` and `m_c_Or` to match commutativity, and adds a second symmetric pattern where the `or` is the right operand. This covers both `B | ((B | C) & A)` and `((B | C) & A) | B` without relying on operand order.

The test suite demonstrates generalization: `PR46712_logical` uses `select` for logical ops, `test45` variants show commuted `and`/`or` operands and extra ops like `mul`, and `test46` confirms the transform works with icmp booleans. The key insight is to match both operand positions and use commutative matchers (`m_c_*`) to cover all permutations, making the optimization robust across frontends and code patterns.
